response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to the protein in peanuts  
the body produces antibodies to fight the protein which can cause severe allergic reactions  
the protein in peanuts is a complex molecule that is not found in water  
the human body is not adapted to live in water and the immune system is not designed to handle it  
the reaction to peanuts can be life-threatening and may lead to anaphylaxis  
the inability to live underwater is a result of the immune system's response to the protein in peanuts
